title: Image Denoising (Gaussian, Poisson, mixed)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence boilerplate. Physics below is grounded in Rudin-Osher-Fatemi (1992) TV denoising; Dabov et al. 2007 BM3D; Zhang et al. 2017 DnCNN; Krull et al. 2019 Noise2Void.

E.description: A clean image x is corrupted by pixelwise noise n(r) whose distribution depends on the capture mode (Gaussian for low-light DSLR, Poisson for photon-shot-noise limited, Rician for MRI, Speckle for SAR/ultrasound).
E.forward_model: y(r) = x(r) + n(r)   OR   y(r) ~ Poisson(x(r))   (pixelwise)
E.world_state_x: Clean image x in R^{H x W x C}, piecewise smooth, sparse gradients
E.observation_y: Noisy image y in R^{H x W x C}
physical_parameters_theta: Noise type (gaussian, poisson, rician, speckle, mixed)
Sensor gain, read noise, dark current

W.regime: Identity operator with additive noise — trivially well-posed in forward sense; inverse relies on prior (TV, non-local, learned). Mismatch primarily in noise-model specification.

C.solver_class: TV (ROF), BM3D, NLM, Wiener, DnCNN, N2N, Noise2Void, Restormer, SwinIR
C.error_bound: ||x_hat - x||_2^2 <= C1 * sigma_n^2 * area + C2 * ||grad x||_1; PSNR saturates near Cramer-Rao bound for Gaussian noise
C.complexity: O(H*W*C) per iteration for local filters; O(H*W*C*P^2) for NLM patch-match; learned single forward
